title: hide() fires the wrong per-id event: 'aos:in:ID' instead of 'aos:out:ID'
body: In src/js/helpers/handleScroll.js, applyClasses()'s hide() function does fireEvent('aos:out', node) for the generic event, but then fires fireEvent(`aos:in:${el.options.id}`, node) for the id-scoped event instead of `aos:out:${el.options.id}`. So listening for `aos:out:my-element` never fires, while `aos:in:my-element` fires twice (once on show, once on hide).

title: MutationObserver fallback message is printed with console.info even when disableMutationObserver is explicitly requested
body: aos.js's init() logs the 'MutationObserver is not supported' console.info message whenever observer.isSupported() is false, but it doesn't first check whether the user already passed disableMutationObserver: true in settings -- so users who intentionally disabled the observer still get the informational warning on unsupported browsers, which is a little noisy.

title: IE11 detection via document.all is fragile for isBrowserNotSupported
body: aos.js gates isBrowserNotSupported() on `document.all && !window.atob`, a well-known IE9-and-below sniff, but some modern automated test environments (headless browsers, certain webviews) also lack window.atob while still supporting document.all-like quirks mode features, causing AOS to disable itself unexpectedly in CI screenshot tests.

title: offsetCalculator does not account for CSS transforms on ancestor elements
body: src/js/helpers/offsetCalculator.js computes an element's offset top by walking offsetParent chains, but if an ancestor has a CSS transform applied (common with modern layout libraries), offsetTop-based calculations become inaccurate because offsetParent semantics change under transformed ancestors, causing aos-animate to trigger at the wrong scroll position.

title: Add a Cypress test for settings_mirror interacting with options.once
body: cypress/integration/settings_mirror.js tests the mirror option and settings_once_spec.js tests once independently, but there's no spec exercising mirror: true together with once: true, which is exactly the combination hit by issue reports about elements re-hiding after being marked as already animated once.
